title: Integrate Documentation Content Rendering
description: Render documentation content dynamically using Markdown or MDX for easy authoring and formatting.
details: Set up Markdown or MDX support in the frontend (e.g., using react-markdown or @mdx-js/react). Load documentation files from a designated directory (e.g., docs/ or public/docs/). Render content within the Documentation page, supporting headings, lists, code blocks, and links. Style rendered content to match the cool UI design.
test_strategy: Add sample Markdown/MDX files and verify they render correctly in the UI. Write tests to ensure content loads and displays as expected.

title: Implement Interactive Features (Search, Navigation Highlighting)
description: Enhance the documentation UI with interactive features such as instant search and active section highlighting.
details: Implement a client-side search/filter for documentation topics. Highlight the active section in the sidebar as the user scrolls or navigates. Use React state/hooks for interactivity. Ensure smooth UX and performance.
test_strategy: Test search functionality with various queries. Verify active section highlighting works as expected. Write unit tests for search logic and navigation state.

title: Create Documentation Page Route and Layout
description: Implement a dedicated route and page structure for the documentation UI in the React frontend.
details: Add a new route (e.g., /docs) in the React frontend under apps/web. Create a main Documentation page component with a modern layout (sidebar navigation, main content area). Use React Router for navigation. Ensure the layout is responsive and visually appealing, following modern UI/UX best practices.
test_strategy: Manually verify the /docs route renders correctly. Check layout on desktop and mobile. Write a Jest/React Testing Library test to ensure the Documentation page mounts and displays expected elements.

title: Design and Implement Stylish Documentation Components
description: Develop reusable, visually appealing components for documentation content (e.g., headings, code blocks, navigation sidebar, search bar).
details: Use a modern component library (e.g., Material UI, Chakra UI, or Tailwind CSS) or custom CSS modules to style documentation elements. Implement components such as a sidebar navigation menu, content sections, code block display, and a search input. Ensure accessibility and theme consistency with the rest of the app.
test_strategy: Visually inspect components for style and responsiveness. Use Jest/React Testing Library to test component rendering and basic interactions (e.g., sidebar navigation, search input).
